name: vague-judge
notes: The judge rubric asks whether the code looks clean and well organized, an unfalsifiable aesthetic judgment with no concrete criteria, so any refactor (including a cosmetic no-op) can be argued to pass.

repoFiles.README.md:
# Config Parser

`parser/config_parser.py` reads a small INI-like config format used by the CLI tool. The current implementation works but grew organically and mixes parsing, validation, and defaulting logic in a few long functions.

contract.goal: Refactor the config parser for readability.

items.description: Split parse_config in parser/config_parser.py into smaller, well-named helper functions that separate section handling, key-value splitting, and value coercion, while preserving its existing behavior.
items.expectedOutput: The refactored parser/config_parser.py is easier to follow than the original.

check.rubric: The refactored code looks clean, readable and well organized
